addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# General Information
2	
3	## Maintainer
4	Patric Bucher [patric.bucher@psi.ch]
5	
6	## Author
7	Oliver Bründler [oli.bruendler@gmx.ch]
8	
9	## License
10	This library is published under [PSI HDL Library License](License.txt), which is [LGPL](LGPL2_1.txt) plus some additional exceptions to clarify the LGPL terms in the context of firmware development.
11	
12	## Changelog
13	See [Changelog](Changelog.md)
14	
15	## Commands
16	See [Command Reference](CommandRef.md)
17	
18	## Features
19	This TCL framework allows easily creating regression tests using modelsim, VHDL and Vivado-Simulator. Files or groups of files can
20	be compiled by a single command, full regression tests can be run easily and results of testbenches can be 
21	parsed automatically.
22	
23	In contrast to modelsim projects and Vivado projects, simulation scripts written using this TCL package are version control friendly and
24	easily mergable.
25	
26	The framework allows running the same simulations using either Modelsim, GHDL or Vivado. See [Command Reference](CommandRef.md) for details.
27	
28	### Notes regarding Vivado
29	Vivado does not support many VHDL-2008 statements and the simulator is quite slow. So only use Vivado if none of the other options work for you. GHDL is faster and supports more VHDL2008 statements.
30	
31	In the PSI-libraries, many testbenches use VHDL 2008, so they cannot be executed with Vivado.
32	
33	## Usage
34	Usually two files are written to describe simulations for a set of VHDL files. 
35	
36	One file is the configuration file (*config.tcl*)
37	and it describes what files, testbenches and runs (testbench with generics and possibly pre- or post-scripts) shall be simulated.
38	
39	The other file is the fun file (*run.tcl*) that can be ran from modelsim console and automatically compile and execute
40	all required simulations.
41	
42	The splitting into two files is very useful since it allows nesting. For example each library can have its *config.tcl* and
43	its *run.tcl*. If one only works on the library, the *run.tcl* of the library can be executed. If one uses the library in a 
44	project, the project *run.tcl* can call the *config.tcl* of the library to include all tests of the library into the project
45	regression test.
46	
47	## Tagging Policy
48	Stable releases are tagged in the form *major*.*minor*.*bugfix*. 
49	
50	* Whenever a change is not fully backward compatible, the *major* version number is incremented
51	* Whenever new features are added, the *minor* version number is incremented
52	* If only bugs are fixed (i.e. no functional changes are applied), the *bugfix* version is incremented
53

# General Information

## Maintainer
Patric Bucher [patric.bucher@psi.ch]

## Author
Oliver Bründler [oli.bruendler@gmx.ch]

## License
This library is published under [PSI HDL Library License](License.txt), which is [LGPL](LGPL2_1.txt) plus some additional exceptions to clarify the LGPL terms in the context of firmware development.

## Changelog
See [Changelog](Changelog.md)

## Commands
See [Command Reference](CommandRef.md)

## Features
This TCL framework allows easily creating regression tests using modelsim, VHDL and Vivado-Simulator. Files or groups of files can
be compiled by a single command, full regression tests can be run easily and results of testbenches can be 
parsed automatically.

In contrast to modelsim projects and Vivado projects, simulation scripts written using this TCL package are version control friendly and
easily mergable.

The framework allows running the same simulations using either Modelsim, GHDL or Vivado. See [Command Reference](CommandRef.md) for details.

### Notes regarding Vivado
Vivado does not support many VHDL-2008 statements and the simulator is quite slow. So only use Vivado if none of the other options work for you. GHDL is faster and supports more VHDL2008 statements.

In the PSI-libraries, many testbenches use VHDL 2008, so they cannot be executed with Vivado.

## Usage
Usually two files are written to describe simulations for a set of VHDL files. 

One file is the configuration file (*config.tcl*)
and it describes what files, testbenches and runs (testbench with generics and possibly pre- or post-scripts) shall be simulated.

The other file is the fun file (*run.tcl*) that can be ran from modelsim console and automatically compile and execute
all required simulations.

The splitting into two files is very useful since it allows nesting. For example each library can have its *config.tcl* and
its *run.tcl*. If one only works on the library, the *run.tcl* of the library can be executed. If one uses the library in a 
project, the project *run.tcl* can call the *config.tcl* of the library to include all tests of the library into the project
regression test.

## Tagging Policy
Stable releases are tagged in the form *major*.*minor*.*bugfix*. 

* Whenever a change is not fully backward compatible, the *major* version number is incremented
* Whenever new features are added, the *minor* version number is incremented
* If only bugs are fixed (i.e. no functional changes are applied), the *bugfix* version is incremented

# Examples

## Example Configuration Script (config.tcl)
```
#Constants
set LibPath "../.."

#Import psi::sim library
namespace import psi::sim::*

#Set library
add_library psi_common

#suppress messages
compile_suppress 135,1236
run_suppress 8684,3479,3813,8009,3812

# psi_tb_v1_0	
add_sources "$LibPath/psi_tb/hdl" {
	psi_tb_txt_util.vhd \
} -tag lib

# Library
add_sources $LibPath {
	psi_tb/hdl/psi_tb_txt_util.vhd \
	psi_tb/hdl/psi_tb_compare_pkg.vhd \
} -tag lib

# project sources
add_sources "../hdl" {
	psi_common_array_pkg.vhd \
	psi_common_math_pkg.vhd \
	psi_common_logic_pkg.vhd \
	psi_common_numeric_std_extension_pkg.vhd \
	psi_common_pulse_cc.vhd \
	psi_common_simple_cc.vhd \
	psi_common_status_cc.vhd \
	psi_common_tdp_ram_rbw.vhd \
	psi_common_sync_fifo.vhd \
	psi_common_async_fifo.vhd \
} -tag src

# testbenches
add_sources "../testbench" {
	psi_common_simple_cc_tb/psi_common_simple_cc_tb.vhd \
	psi_common_status_cc_tb/psi_common_status_cc_tb.vhd \
	psi_common_sync_fifo_tb/psi_common_sync_fifo_tb.vhd \
	psi_common_async_fifo_tb/psi_common_async_fifo_tb.vhd \
	psi_common_logic_pkg_tb/psi_common_logic_pkg_tb.vhd \
} -tag tb
	
#TB Runs
create_tb_run "psi_common_simple_cc_tb"
tb_run_add_arguments \
	"-gClockRatio_g=3" \
	"-gClockRatio_g=1.01" \
	"-gClockRatio_g=0.99" \
	"-gClockRatio_g=0.3"
add_tb_run

create_tb_run "psi_common_status_cc_tb"
tb_run_add_arguments \
	"-gClockRatio_g=3" \
	"-gClockRatio_g=1.01" \
	"-gClockRatio_g=0.99" \
	"-gClockRatio_g=0.3"
add_tb_run

create_tb_run "psi_common_sync_fifo_tb"
tb_run_add_arguments \
	"-gAlmFullOn_g=true -gAlmEmptyOn_g=true -gDepth_g=32" \
	"-gAlmFullOn_g=false -gAlmEmptyOn_g=false -gDepth_g=128"
add_tb_run

create_tb_run "psi_common_async_fifo_tb"
tb_run_add_arguments \
	"-gAlmFullOn_g=true -gAlmEmptyOn_g=true -gDepth_g=32" \
	"-gAlmFullOn_g=false -gAlmEmptyOn_g=false -gDepth_g=128"
add_tb_run

create_tb_run "psi_common_logic_pkg_tb"
add_tb_run
```

## Example Execution Script (run.tcl)  
```
#Load dependencies
source ../../../TCL/PsiSim/PsiSim.tcl

#Import psi::sim library
namespace import psi::sim::*

#Initialize Simulation
init

#Configure
source ./config.tcl

#Run Simulation
puts "------------------------------"
puts "-- Compile"
puts "------------------------------"
compile_files -all -clean
puts "------------------------------"
puts "-- Run"
puts "------------------------------"
run_tb -all
puts "------------------------------"
puts "-- Check"
puts "------------------------------"

## 2.5.0

* Added Features
  * Support 'glob' patterns in add_sources.
* Bugfixes
  * GHDL: work-around for language version 2002
  * GHDL: install library products into subdirs
  * Vivado: fixed 'exec xvhdl ... $langArg ...' command (confused by empty langArg)

## 2.4.0

* Added Features
  * GHDL support for launch\_tb
  * GTK support for launch\_tb
  * Modelsim Waveform Format support (*.do)
  * Modelsim Quick Start Waveform with -wave option without addtional arguments
* Bugfixes
  * Changed compile settings for GHDL to place data in library name named folder
  * Make launch_tb with default arguments work (pass empty string)
  * Option combinations in launch\_tb did not work when -wave was used without filename
  
## 2.3.1

* Bugfixes
  * Made *tb\_run\_skip* command working for multiple simulators

## 2.3.0

* Added Features
  * Added *launch\_tb* command
  * Show message icons in Modelsim
* Bugfixes
  * Made GHDL more permissive to better match Modelsim behavior

## 2.2.0

* Added Features
  * Vivado simulator is now also supported

## 2.1.0

* Added Features
  * Added *tb\_run\_skip* - possibility to skip a simulation for one or all simulator tools (helps avoiding tool bugs)

## 2.0.2

* Bugfixes
  * Fixed Modelsim Version Readout (required for 2.0.1) to also work on linux and be more stable

## 2.0.1

* Bugfixes
  * Do only use -novopt flag for modelsim versions < 10.7 (does not exist anymore in newer versions)

## 2.0.0

* First open-source release (older history is not kept)

## 1.5.1

* Bugfixes
  * Version and Options (added in 1.5.0) did not always work due to TCL/Modelsim issues
  * Only print warning (and do not abort with an error) if the same file is added twice

## 1.5.0

* Added Features
  * Version and Options switch added to add\_sources command.
  * Check if the same file is added to the same library multiple times and throw error if this is the case

## 1.4.0

* Added Features
  * PsiSim now also supports GHDL (not only Modelsim). In the case of GHDL, the GHDL directory must be added to the system path and the TCL scripts must be evaluated by a standalone TCL interpreter (e.g. active TCL).

## 1.3.1

* Bugfixes
  * Prevented simulation from aborting if the transcript file cannot be deleted. This is the case for batch-mode execution where the option *-logfile transcript.transcript* must be passed to the *vsim* command and therefore this file is locked during execution of the simulation.

## 1.3.0

* Added features
  * Added -contains option to *run_tb* and *compile* commands to only handle files with a given string in their name (helps with development focused on some parts of bigger libraries)

## 1.2.0

* Added Features
  * Added the option to limit the simulation time for each tb\_run (to allow using testbenches that do not stop on their own)
* Bugfixes
  * None

## 1.1.1

* Added Features

* Bugfixes
  * Fix issue #1: run\_check_errors did not work when run\_tb was not run previously
  * Fix issue #2: compile\_files ignored arguments

## V1.01

* Added Features
  * Implemented propper namespace handling
* Bugfixes
  * None

## V1.00

* First release

input.rationale: PsiSim 是一个单文件 TCL 框架（PsiSim.tcl，约 966 行），但概念密度高：它用 psi::sim 命名空间封装了一整套 VHDL 回归测试流程，内部由两层构成——面向用户的接口函数和屏蔽三种仿真器（Modelsim/GHDL/Vivado）差异的 SAL 抽象层，并维护 Sources 与 TbRuns 两个核心数据模型。因此学习路线按"先用起来→理解配置与数据模型→深入 SAL 机制→扩展与实践"四层递进设计。单元 1（入门）帮助读者从零建立认知：项目定位、仓库结构、两文件工作流（config.tcl + run.tcl）、命令全景。单元 2（进阶）拆解配置阶段的核心：命名空间与状态变量、Sources/TbRuns 两个 dict 数据模型、消息抑制、编译过滤、run_tb 遍历与脚本钩子、错误检查。单元 3（专家）专门讲 SAL 的 dispatch 模式，逐层剖析 transcript/日志/版本处理、编译抽象、仿真运行抽象和交互调试。单元 4（专家）面向二次开发与最佳实践：嵌套多库工程、GHDL/GTKWave 完整工作流、扩展新仿真器与架构取舍。讲义依赖形成无环 DAG，保证读者可按序逐篇阅读，每篇只解决一个独立问题并配有结合真实源码的代码实践任务。

minimal_modules: 命令与源码 proc 的对应关系

units.title: 进阶：核心数据模型与配置链路

lectures.title: 命名空间、状态变量与 init
lectures.topic: 深入 psi::sim 命名空间的实现：namespace eval/variable/export 机制、全部状态变量的含义，以及 init 命令如何选择仿真器并重置状态。
learning_goals: 理解 TCL namespace eval/variable/export 在 PsiSim 中的用法
掌握全部状态变量 (Libraries/Sources/TbRuns/CompileSuppress/RunSuppress/Simulator 等) 的作用
读懂 init 的参数解析 (-ghdl/-vivado) 与状态重置逻辑
minimal_modules: init 的参数解析与状态重置
lectures.practice_task: 在 PsiSim.tcl 中为每个状态变量写一句注释说明它存储什么，然后解释为什么 init 必须是脚本里第一个被调用的命令。

lectures.title: 库与源文件管理（Sources 数据模型）
lectures.topic: add_library 与 add_sources 如何构建 Sources 数据模型：每个源文件是一个 dict (PATH/LIBRARY/TAG/LANGUAGE/VERSION/OPTIONS)，以及 add_sources 的参数解析与 glob 通配符支持。
learning_goals: 掌握 Sources 列表中每个 dict 的结构
理解 add_sources 的 -lib/-tag/-language/-version/-options 参数解析
学会使用 glob 模式批量添加文件并理解文件去重警告
lectures.practice_task: 写一段 add_sources 调用，用 glob 模式一次性把某目录下所有 .vhd 文件加到指定库并打上 -tag src；然后用 dict 的思路画出添加后 Sources 中一个元素的结构。

lectures.title: 测试运行定义（TbRuns 数据模型）
lectures.topic: create_tb_run/add_tb_run 及夹在中间的配置命令 (tb_run_add_arguments/pre_script/post_script/time_limit/skip) 如何构建 ThisTbRun 并最终落入 TbRuns。
learning_goals: 掌握 ThisTbRun 与 TbRuns 的 dict 字段结构

lectures.title: 消息抑制机制

learning_goals: 理解 compile_suppress/run_suppress 的字符串拼接实现
掌握用 regexp 做去重的逻辑
了解抑制字符串如何衔接到编译/运行流程
minimal_modules: 消息编号的拼接与去重

lectures.title: 编译流程与过滤
lectures.topic: compile_files（内部 compile）与 clean_libraries 如何遍历 Sources，按 -all/-lib/-tag/-contains 过滤后调用 SAL 的 sal_compile_file。
learning_goals: 掌握 compile 命令的多维过滤逻辑

了解 clean_libraries 的清理机制
lectures.practice_task: 解释 compile_files -all -tag src -contains fifo 会编译哪些文件，并说明为什么同时存在 compile 和 compile_files 两个名字。

lectures.title: 仿真运行与脚本钩子（run_tb）
lectures.topic: run_tb 如何遍历 TbRuns，按 -all/-lib/-name/-contains 过滤、处理 skip、执行 pre/post script，并对每组 tb_args 调用 sal_run_tb。
learning_goals: 掌握 run_tb 的过滤与遍历逻辑

lectures.title: 错误检查与 transcript
lectures.topic: run_check_errors 如何读取 Transcript.transcript，用正则匹配错误字符串（含 Fatal:）判定回归是否通过，以及为什么推荐用 ###ERROR### 这种独特模式。
learning_goals: 理解 run_check_errors 的正则匹配逻辑

lectures.title: SAL 设计与 dispatch 模式

掌握 if/elseif dispatch 模式与 Simulator 变量
认识 SAL 中统一的错误处理范式（Unsupported Simulator）

learning_goals: 掌握 transcript 文件机制与 batch_mode 处理

lectures.practice_task: 解释 sal_clean_transcript 在 batch mode 下行为为何不同，并跟踪 sal_init_simulator 如何用正则从 vcom -version 的输出中提取版本号。

lectures.title: 编译抽象（sal_compile_file / sal_clean_lib）
lectures.topic: sal_compile_file 如何把统一的 (lib/path/language/version/options) 映射到 Modelsim vcom/vlog、GHDL ghdl -a（含 2002/2008 双编译）、Vivado xvhdl；sal_clean_lib 如何清理库目录。
learning_goals: 掌握三种仿真器编译命令的差异
理解 GHDL 2002/2008 双编译与 --workdir 子目录机制
了解 Vivado xvhdl 的 --2008 与用 eval 处理空 langArg
lectures.practice_task: 对同一个 VHDL-2008 文件，写出 Modelsim/GHDL/Vivado 三种仿真器在 sal_compile_file 中实际执行的具体命令，并解释 GHDL 为何要同时编译 2002 和 2008。

lectures.title: 仿真运行抽象（sal_run_tb）
lectures.topic: sal_run_tb 如何把统一的 TB 运行映射到 Modelsim vsim、GHDL --elab-run（含 --stop-time/--wave）、Vivado xelab+xsim（含 initfile workaround 与 generic 转换）。
learning_goals: 掌握三种仿真器的仿真运行命令
理解 Vivado 的 initfile workaround 与 -generic_top 转换
了解 GHDL 的 stop-time 与波形参数处理
minimal_modules: Vivado xelab/xsim 与 generic 转换
lectures.practice_task: 跟踪一次带 generics (-gClockRatio_g=3) 和时间限制的 sal_run_tb，写出 Vivado 分支如何把 -g 参数转成 -generic_top，以及生成的 psi_sim_run.tcl 内容。

lectures.title: 交互调试（launch_tb / 波形）

learning_goals: 掌握 launch_tb 的参数解析与 -wave/-show 组合处理
理解 Modelsim 与 GHDL 调试路径的差异
学会 GHDL/GTKWave 的迭代调试工作流
minimal_modules: Modelsim 交互 vs GHDL 波形
lectures.practice_task: 描述 launch_tb -contains fifo -argidx 1 -wave -show 在 Modelsim 和 GHDL 下分别会发生什么，并复述 CommandRef 中的 GHDL/GTK 迭代调试步骤。

units.title: 专家：扩展、实践与架构取舍

lectures.title: 嵌套配置与多库项目实践
lectures.topic: 如何利用 config.tcl/run.tcl 拆分实现库级与项目级嵌套回归，结合 add_library 多库、-tag 分组编译、-contains 局部编译，构建可维护的多库仿真工程。
learning_goals: 掌握 config.tcl/run.tcl 嵌套调用模式
学会多库工程的组织与 -tag 分组
理解 -contains 局部编译如何加速开发迭代
lectures.practice_task: 设计一个含两个子库 (psi_common, psi_tb) 的项目结构：每个子库有自己的 config.tcl/run.tcl，顶层 run.tcl 通过 source 它们的 config.tcl 汇总回归。写出关键文件内容草图。

lectures.title: GHDL/GTKWave 工作流深度实践
lectures.topic: 完整跑通 GHDL 仿真：用独立 tclsh（如 Active TCL）运行脚本、理解 GHDL 2002/2008 双编译与库产物子目录、.ghw 波形文件、GTKWave 迭代调试，以及 synopsys ieee 库的使用。
learning_goals: 掌握 GHDL 独立 tclsh 运行环境要求
理解 GHDL 双版本编译与库产物子目录机制
学会用 .ghw + GTKWave 完成迭代调试
minimal_modules: 双版本编译与库子目录产物
lectures.practice_task: 写一份 GHDL 模式下的 run.tcl (init -ghdl)，说明它与 Modelsim 版 run.tcl 的唯一区别，并解释为什么 GHDL 用 .ghw 而非 .vcd（参见 Changelog 中 records 修复记录）。

lectures.title: 扩展新模拟器与架构取舍
lectures.topic: 如何为 PsiSim 增加一个全新仿真器支持（扩展 init 及所有 sal_* proc 的 dispatch 分支、sal_launch_tb/sal_open_wave），以及当前架构的取舍：全局可变状态、eval 字符串拼接、错误处理范式、测试缺失。
learning_goals: 掌握添加新仿真器需要改动的全部位置
理解 SAL dispatch 的扩展点与限制
认识架构层面的取舍与潜在改进方向
lectures.practice_task: 假设要支持一个名为 'Icarus' 的仿真器，列出你需要修改的所有 proc 及各自要加的分支，并指出当前'全局可变状态 + eval 拼接命令'带来的至少两个风险。

data.head: 434f6a9bd8c79c7d20522344996635516fc95392
data.rationale: PsiSim 是一个单文件 TCL 框架（PsiSim.tcl，约 966 行），但概念密度高：它用 psi::sim 命名空间封装了一整套 VHDL 回归测试流程，内部由两层构成——面向用户的接口函数和屏蔽三种仿真器（Modelsim/GHDL/Vivado）差异的 SAL 抽象层，并维护 Sources 与 TbRuns 两个核心数据模型。因此学习路线按"先用起来→理解配置与数据模型→深入 SAL 机制→扩展与实践"四层递进设计。单元 1（入门）帮助读者从零建立认知：项目定位、仓库结构、两文件工作流（config.tcl + run.tcl）、命令全景。单元 2（进阶）拆解配置阶段的核心：命名空间与状态变量、Sources/TbRuns 两个 dict 数据模型、消息抑制、编译过滤、run_tb 遍历与脚本钩子、错误检查。单元 3（专家）专门讲 SAL 的 dispatch 模式，逐层剖析 transcript/日志/版本处理、编译抽象、仿真运行抽象和交互调试。单元 4（专家）面向二次开发与最佳实践：嵌套多库工程、GHDL/GTKWave 完整工作流、扩展新仿真器与架构取舍。讲义依赖形成无环 DAG，保证读者可按序逐篇阅读，每篇只解决一个独立问题并配有结合真实源码的代码实践任务。

lectures.topic: run_check_errors 如何读取 Transcript.transcript，用正则匹配错误字符串（含 Fatal:）判定回归是否通过，以及为什么推荐用 ###ERROR### 这种独特模式。
learning_goals: 理解 run_check_errors 的正则匹配逻辑
掌握错误模式选择原则以避免误报
了解 transcript 文件的作用与命令行过滤
minimal_modules: transcript 读取与命令行过滤
lectures.practice_task: 构造一段同时含 'Checking zero Position Error' 和 '###ERROR###' 的假 transcript，运行 run_check_errors '###ERROR###'，并解释为什么用 'Error' 作模式会误判。

lectures.title: SAL 设计与 dispatch 模式
lectures.topic: 模拟器抽象层（SAL）的设计思想：用 Simulator 变量在每个 sal_* proc 内 if/elseif dispatch 到 Modelsim/GHDL/Vivado 三套实现，统一接口屏蔽差异。
learning_goals: 理解 SAL 的抽象目的与边界
掌握 if/elseif dispatch 模式与 Simulator 变量
认识 SAL 中统一的错误处理范式（Unsupported Simulator）
minimal_modules: dispatch 模式与 Simulator 变量
lectures.practice_task: 列出所有 sal_* proc，按'仅 Modelsim / Modelsim+GHDL+Vivado 共用 / GHDL 独有'分类，并说明 dispatch 的判断依据。

lectures.title: transcript、日志与版本处理
lectures.topic: sal_print_log / transcript 系列（off/on/set_file/clean）如何统一日志输出，sal_init_simulator 如何探测 Modelsim 版本，sal_version_specific_flags 如何按版本返回 -novopt。
learning_goals: 掌握 transcript 文件机制与 batch_mode 处理
理解 Modelsim 版本探测的'文件中转'技巧
了解版本相关 flag 的返回逻辑
lectures.practice_task: 解释 sal_clean_transcript 在 batch mode 下行为为何不同，并跟踪 sal_init_simulator 如何用正则从 vcom -version 的输出中提取版本号。

lectures.title: 编译抽象（sal_compile_file / sal_clean_lib）
lectures.topic: sal_compile_file 如何把统一的 (lib/path/language/version/options) 映射到 Modelsim vcom/vlog、GHDL ghdl -a（含 2002/2008 双编译）、Vivado xvhdl；sal_clean_lib 如何清理库目录。
learning_goals: 掌握三种仿真器编译命令的差异
理解 GHDL 2002/2008 双编译与 --workdir 子目录机制
了解 Vivado xvhdl 的 --2008 与用 eval 处理空 langArg
lectures.practice_task: 对同一个 VHDL-2008 文件，写出 Modelsim/GHDL/Vivado 三种仿真器在 sal_compile_file 中实际执行的具体命令，并解释 GHDL 为何要同时编译 2002 和 2008。

lectures.title: 仿真运行抽象（sal_run_tb）
lectures.topic: sal_run_tb 如何把统一的 TB 运行映射到 Modelsim vsim、GHDL --elab-run（含 --stop-time/--wave）、Vivado xelab+xsim（含 initfile workaround 与 generic 转换）。
learning_goals: 掌握三种仿真器的仿真运行命令
理解 Vivado 的 initfile workaround 与 -generic_top 转换
了解 GHDL 的 stop-time 与波形参数处理
minimal_modules: Vivado xelab/xsim 与 generic 转换
lectures.practice_task: 跟踪一次带 generics (-gClockRatio_g=3) 和时间限制的 sal_run_tb，写出 Vivado 分支如何把 -g 参数转成 -generic_top，以及生成的 psi_sim_run.tcl 内容。

lectures.title: 交互调试（launch_tb / 波形）
lectures.topic: launch_tb 如何启动（但不执行）一个 TB 用于交互调试，参数解析 (-contains/-argidx/-wave/-show)，以及 Modelsim (sal_launch_tb) 与 GHDL (.ghw 波形 + sal_open_wave GTKWave) 的差异和推荐工作流。
learning_goals: 掌握 launch_tb 的参数解析与 -wave/-show 组合处理
理解 Modelsim 与 GHDL 调试路径的差异
学会 GHDL/GTKWave 的迭代调试工作流
minimal_modules: Modelsim 交互 vs GHDL 波形
lectures.practice_task: 描述 launch_tb -contains fifo -argidx 1 -wave -show 在 Modelsim 和 GHDL 下分别会发生什么，并复述 CommandRef 中的 GHDL/GTK 迭代调试步骤。

units.title: 专家：扩展、实践与架构取舍

lectures.title: 嵌套配置与多库项目实践
lectures.topic: 如何利用 config.tcl/run.tcl 拆分实现库级与项目级嵌套回归，结合 add_library 多库、-tag 分组编译、-contains 局部编译，构建可维护的多库仿真工程。
learning_goals: 掌握 config.tcl/run.tcl 嵌套调用模式
学会多库工程的组织与 -tag 分组
理解 -contains 局部编译如何加速开发迭代
lectures.practice_task: 设计一个含两个子库 (psi_common, psi_tb) 的项目结构：每个子库有自己的 config.tcl/run.tcl，顶层 run.tcl 通过 source 它们的 config.tcl 汇总回归。写出关键文件内容草图。

lectures.title: GHDL/GTKWave 工作流深度实践
lectures.topic: 完整跑通 GHDL 仿真：用独立 tclsh（如 Active TCL）运行脚本、理解 GHDL 2002/2008 双编译与库产物子目录、.ghw 波形文件、GTKWave 迭代调试，以及 synopsys ieee 库的使用。
learning_goals: 掌握 GHDL 独立 tclsh 运行环境要求
理解 GHDL 双版本编译与库产物子目录机制
学会用 .ghw + GTKWave 完成迭代调试
minimal_modules: 双版本编译与库子目录产物
lectures.practice_task: 写一份 GHDL 模式下的 run.tcl (init -ghdl)，说明它与 Modelsim 版 run.tcl 的唯一区别，并解释为什么 GHDL 用 .ghw 而非 .vcd（参见 Changelog 中 records 修复记录）。

lectures.title: 扩展新模拟器与架构取舍
lectures.topic: 如何为 PsiSim 增加一个全新仿真器支持（扩展 init 及所有 sal_* proc 的 dispatch 分支、sal_launch_tb/sal_open_wave），以及当前架构的取舍：全局可变状态、eval 字符串拼接、错误处理范式、测试缺失。
learning_goals: 掌握添加新仿真器需要改动的全部位置
理解 SAL dispatch 的扩展点与限制
认识架构层面的取舍与潜在改进方向
lectures.practice_task: 假设要支持一个名为 'Icarus' 的仿真器，列出你需要修改的所有 proc 及各自要加的分支，并指出当前'全局可变状态 + eval 拼接命令'带来的至少两个风险。
